title: OpenCode Command Contract
description: Versioned, machine-readable behavioral contract for the OpenCode command families. One entry per family router captures the behavioral truth previously duplicated as prose across the skill, the templates, the router prose, the workflow YAML, the runtime, the mirrors, and the benchmark adapters. Downstream consumers (templates, validators, benchmark adapters, generated routers) read this instead of restating it.

version.description: Contract semver. Bump minor when adding an optional field, major on a breaking field change.

metadata.description: Non-normative provenance and cross-cutting notes (e.g. the observed confirm/auto relationship, which is family-dependent rather than a universal invariant).

families.description: Contract entries keyed by family name (create, design, speckit, memory, doctor, deep).

topology.description: One of four top-level classes. A manifest-backed subaction router (doctor) is a subtype of subaction-route-manifest, not a fifth class.

router_path.description: Repo-relative path(s) of the family's router .md file(s). Families with per-operation routers (create, design, deep) carry an array.

required.description: Whether the command requires user input to proceed.

gate_owner.description: Where a required input is gated. A required input is always router-gated or target-gated — never ungated. 'none' is only valid when required is false.

argument_hint.description: The frontmatter argument-hint the router advertises.

execution_targets.description: Each selector (mode suffix or subaction) mapped to the asset, script, tool, or route-manifest entry it hands off to.

selector.description: Mode suffix (:auto), subaction name, or the default selector.

target.description: The asset path, script, tool, or route-manifest entry the selector executes.

default_policy.description: How the family behaves for a complete invocation with no explicit mode suffix. confirm = ask before acting; conditional-auto = act autonomously when inputs are complete, else ask; ask = require an explicit mode choice; confirm-only = the only mode is confirm; non-mutating-default = a direct-dispatch family whose bare invocation is read-only/preview and needs an explicit apply to mutate.

supported_modes.description: The mode suffixes the family accepts (e.g. :auto, :confirm), or subaction names for a subaction router. Empty for a direct-dispatch family with no mode suffixes.

overrides.description: Per-command deviations from the family default (e.g. a family that is 'ask' by default but 'confirm' for one command).

owned_assets.description: The assets the family owns. A mode-pair family owns the presentation .txt plus the _auto.yaml / _confirm.yaml pair.

path.description: Repo-relative asset path, or a template placeholder for the family shape.

loader_requirements.description: Anything the OpenCode/Claude loader needs beyond the .md (sidecar files, symlinks, $ARGUMENTS handling).

owner.description: Which surface owns user-facing presentation. Canonically the presentation asset; router-inline is only sanctioned via a typed exception.

exceptions.description: Sanctioned inline presentation (render-fidelity), each pinned to the command and surface where it is allowed, so an intent-aware check can pass it without a blanket allowlist.

surface.description: The anchor, section, or output surface where the inline presentation is sanctioned.

gated.description: Whether destructive operations are behind a confirmation gate.

operations.description: The destructive/irreversible operations the family can perform.

timeout_bounds.description: Documented timeout or step bounds, or null when the family sets none.

workflow_schema_ref.description: For a mode-pair family, the shared command-workflow schema its _auto/_confirm YAMLs conform to (confirm == auto + checkpoints). Null for non-workflow topologies.

routing_source.description: Subaction-route-manifest subtype (populated by the doctor-subtype phase): where routes come from. Absent for other topologies.

compiled_contract_marker.description: Marker string a compiled-stub router carries; exempts it from authored-section requirements.
